Fern Polrrrcer- PRACrrcEs CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95g14-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322_0886
August 3, 2010
Doug Goto
164 Bayline Circle
Folsom, CA 95630
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. I-10-ll8
Dear Mr. Goto:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the post-governmental
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the *Act"[t *' baJe this letter on the facrs
presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as the finder of fact when it
renders assistance' (ln re oglesby (1975) I FPPC ops. 71.) Because your questions are general
in nature and do not involve specific governmental dicisions or outsidL positions, we can
provide you only with infbrmal assistance.2 Finally, we only provicle advice under the provisions
of the Act, and therefbreoffer no opinion on the application, if ony, of other laws such as public
Contract Code Section l04l l.
QUESTION
what restrictions, if any, apply to you should you find and accept a consulting position
now that you have been out of state service for more ihan one year?
CONCLUSION
The one-year ban does not apply to yoll, as explained below. The permanent ban woulcl
apply in specific circumstances, also clescribed below. You will need to consider your particular
situation should you start consulting with firms that contract with the State.
' The Political Reftrrm Act is sontained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ret'erences are to the Gor''ernment code. unless <itherwise indicated. The regulations 0f the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections I8ll0 through llt997 of Title 2 of thi Califirrnia Code of Regulations. All
re-uulattlry ret'erences are to'ritle 2. Division 6 0lthe California Cocle of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
r Intrlrmal assistance does not provide the requesbr with the immunity provicled by an opinion or tirrmal
written advice. (Section 831 I-l; Regularion lg-129(cX j).)
":ia


File No. I-10-l l8
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are a former state employee who retired from state service inZO}9. you are now
interested in becoming a consultant or subcontracting consultant with clients that do business
with the State of California. Your services would be in information technology, business
instruction, writing, or project management.
You retired from the Department of Social Services on April 15,z11g,as a Senior
Information Systems Analyst. Previons positions included office Chief Staff Services Manager
II for the Department of Transportation. You filed a Statement of Economic lnterests (Form
700) for these positions.
While you retired from state service on April 15,20og,your leave credit did not run out
until october 31, 2009. After october 2010, you plan to apply ior consulting positions.
AIIALYSIs
Post-Governmental Employment Restrictions
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two types of post-governmental
employment provisions under the Act, colloquially kntwn as the 'ilvolving door,, prohibitions.
one Year Ban: This ban prohibits a public official from appearing for compensation
before his or her former agency, or officer oi employee thereof, for the purpose of influencing
any administrative, legislative or other specified i"tion (including 
"ortru"tr;. This ban applie"s
for 12 months from jhe date the employee permanently leaves his or her agency. (Regulation
L8746'1(bXl) and (2).) Additionally, the date an "-pioy". 
permanently lJaves office or
employment is the date on which that employee ceases io perform the duties of the office or
employment. (Regulation 18746.4(b).) one has left permanently if one is merely receiving
compensation for leave credits.
You left your state position permanently on April t5,2o}g. This ban does not apply to
you as it has been 12 months since your date of separation.
Permanent Ban: This ban prohibits a former state administrative official from advising or
representing any person, other than the State of California, for compensation in any judicial,
quasi-judicial or other groceeding in which the official participated while serving as a srate
administrative official .' (see Sections g7 40 I -97 402, regulation t sz+ t. t.)
The permanent ban is a lifetime ban and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or other
proceeding in which you participated while you seived as a srate administrative official.
"'Judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding' means any proceeding, application, request for a
I "state Administrative otficial means every member, offlcer, employee or consultant of a state
administrative agency who as part of his or her otllcial responsibilities engages in any judicial, quasi-judicial, or
<lther proceeding in other than a purely clerical. secretarial, or ministerial capacity." (Section g7400(b).)


File No. I-10-118
Page No. 3
ruling or other determination, contract, claim, controversy, investigation, charge, accusation,
arrest or other particular matter involving a specific party or partiei in any court or state
administrative agency . . .." (Section 87400(L).) enorricial ls considered to have ..participated,,
in a proceeding if he. or she took part in the proceeding "personally, and substantially through
decision, approval, disapproval, formal writien.."ornir"ndation, iendering aJuice on a
substantial basis, investigation, or use of confidential information . . ..,, lsZ.tion g7400(d).)
"The permanent ban does not apply to a 'new' proceeding even in cases where the new
proceeding is related to or grows ottt of a prior pro"".d-ing in which the official had participated.
A 'new' proceeding not subject to the p".rnar"nt ban typ[ally involves different parties, a
different subject matter, or different factual issues fromihose considered in previous
proceedings"' (Ris/ Advice Letter, No. A-04-187; also see Donovan Advice Letter, No. I-03-
I l9') New contracts-with the employee's former agency in which the former employee did not
participate are considered new proceedings. (Lestiiadvice Letter, No. I-g9-649.) A new
contract is one that is based on new consideration and new terms, even if it involves the same
parties' (Ferber Advice Letter, No. I-99-104; Anclerson Advice Letter, No. A-9g-159.) In
addition, the application, drafting, and awarding of a contract, license, or approval is considered
to be a proceeding separate from the monitoring and performance of the contract, license, or
approval. (Anderson, supra; BlonienAdvice Litter, No. A-g9-a63.)
while we have detailed the general provisions of the permanent ban for your review, you
have not provided any information as to_your participation in any pro"".airgit o, *uy affect
your ability to engage in any of the conduct liited heiein. To apply the permanent ban to your
situation, you need to determine if any of the actions in which you may engage with any
potential employer or contractor involve a proceeding in which yorl partici[o|"0 *rrit" working
for the Department of Social Services or th; Department of Transpoitation'(Regulation 
- --;---D
t87arJ(a)@).)
If you determine that you participated in any proceeding that may be subject to the
permanent ban in your post-governmental employm"nt, pl"or" feel free to write for further
assistance after providing the relevant facts.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (gt6) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
ilr,5l.l* It{l'^F""-
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
